review_text: Great Park. Large sites. Wonderful amenities, great camp kitchen. Loved the indoor heated pool/spa.
The unit was modern, spacious and clean. Good location. We enjoyed our stay.

Lovely clean park with a lake across the road. The warm floor in the bathroom was a winner!!!!
Friendly staff.
Recommend this especially if you are interested in going to Sovereign Hill - a short walk away.
Nice, well run caravan park, plenty if facilities and lots to do for kids
To many spiders on the outside of the building and there were lots of marks on blinds & ceiling from what appeared to be dead insects.

Fantastic place to stay if you have kids. Unfortunately I don't have kids & am located next to the jumping pillow, which is the kids gathering place & sounds like thunder when they jump on it! Luckily the pillow is deflated at night.
The location is great, very close to Sovereign Hill.
The camp site is really clean and tidy. Has all the facilities you would want and more :)
Clean, perfect to visit Sovereign Hill
Beautiful, well maintained park. Friendly staff and nice big sites. Will definitely be back 👍

When it’s peak season they should check the toilets more than once a day. No toilet paper in some stalls by late afternoon and in need of a good clean. They also don’t mind charging extra when you leave 15 minutes past check out.

Well worth the money and worth the stay!! Yes there was some down falls and that was no wifi, some windows where dirty still. Oh and bed sheets had stains on them otherwise fantastic stay and well looked after cabins
We picked this park for it's location to Sovereign Hill. It was a bit tight and very busy but extremely clean and tidy

This was honestly one of the worst stays we’ve had, and I would not recommend this park to anyone.
While the reception staff were friendly on arrival, the condition of the cabin was completely unacceptable. As soon as we opened the door, there was a strong, unpleasant smell. It was obvious straight away that the cabin had not been properly cleaned. The benches were sticky, there were crumbs and sauce left in the fridge, the shower had orange grime built up in the cracks, and the toilets were not cleaned properly.
The overall cleanliness was shocking. The fly screen in the toilet was so dusty I could literally write “clean me” on it with my finger. The vacuum was completely full, so I had to empty it myself just to vacuum the cabin when we arrived. The washing machine also smelled like it hadn’t been cleaned in a long time.
The bedding was just as bad. The electric blanket didn’t even fit the bed properly, making it uncomfortable to sleep on, and the mattress itself was extremely uncomfortable. I ended up having to go out and buy extra bedding just to get a decent night’s sleep.
We specifically booked this park because of the giant chess set and jumping pillow advertised online, as that’s what our kids were most excited about. When we asked about them, we were told they weren’t operating due to damage and theft. This was never disclosed prior to booking. If key facilities are unavailable, guests should be informed or compensated — it felt very misleading.
The pool area was also disgusting. The pool itself was visibly dirty, especially along the bottom, and the surrounding area was covered in duck droppings. Ducks come in at night, swim in the pool, and leave mess everywhere. By the morning, the area is filthy and completely unhygienic.
The indoor spa was no better — while it sounds good in theory, the chemical levels were so strong that it actually burned your eyes just sitting there, especially in the mornings from the fumes.
We were also shocked to see large trucks, excavators, and heavy machinery parked throughout the caravan park. This felt extremely unsafe, especially with children around, and is not something you expect at a family holiday park.
We were told there was an arcade/games room, which there is, but it ended up costing us $50 on the first day alone for two kids, which adds up quickly.
To top it off, our shower flooded on the first day. Maintenance came the next day and gave us a bottle of wine and a few tokens, but that doesn’t make up for the overall poor standard of accommodation.
The outdoor playground looked extremely old and poorly maintained, with broken equipment that felt unsafe. The indoor play area was only suitable for toddlers, so there was very little for older kids to do.
Overall, this place is poorly maintained, unhygienic, and not as advertised. The lack of cleanliness alone is unacceptable, and the failure to disclose unavailable facilities is extremely disappointing.
We will not be returning.
This is a wonder park that can handle big vans so pleased about that, 31’ most here in Ballarat said no. Recommend you take time in the heated Spa!!!

Absolutely recommend this park
Kids loved it so much to do and see
Stayed in a lot of holiday parks, but will say this is one of the cleanest, and friendliest i have Stayed in, great location and friendly staff made the long weekend stay worth it!

My daughter booked this accommodation as we came down from Qld to attend my father's memorial service, it was a very stressful time and it was nice to come back to the cabin, which was so clean and comfortable, thank you.
Great for visiting key spots in the city.
Great park good amenities, Loved staying here

Excellent service and installation. The place has a spa, hot showers, kitchen, laundry.
Good value for money.

Absolutely lovely holiday park

Highly recommended

Fantastic place to stay ! I have a huge bus and was able to get in without a problem - super friendly staff - great amenities and I also got a room for a fiend that came to visit and they are awesome !!!! Best place around Ballarat to stay for sure
Easy, clean and quiet.
Lovely place to stay & great pool!!
Great holiday park, amenities always clean, nice pool, great swim spa, jumping pillow, tennis court, and even entertainment at the camp kitchen. It's got the lot.
Staff are super friendly and helpful, great facilities, great atmosphere. Would highly recommend a stay

It's a great park. Amenities are nice and clean the camp kitchen is a great area with a big screen TV for watching sports. The van sites and are of a good size but the concrete pads that are there when putting your wheels on the area that's for them your tow hitch is on the road. The wifi no matter what time of day is useless most times it times out so didn't use it. The entrance into the park off the road is shocking full of potholes just as bad as the rest of the roads in Victoria.

Started staying here about 4 years ago, always prebook the same spot for same time next year, it's that good.

Friendly staff. Coffee and ice cream vans come to the park every other day, and on holiday periods, staff organise family friendly entertainment.

Safe environment for the young kids to run free as all visitors understand kiddies have right of way. So quiet at night could hear a pin drop.

Cooking and toilet facilities clean and well spread out.
Accidental booking could not be refunded even by canceling directly same day.

We spent one night in NRMA holiday park, it was a really nice experience in a very well-run, nice quality and service campsite, better than the other BIG4 holiday parks I've previously been to and can't get any better. The location was perfect surrounded by peaceful neighbours (ok, maybe not, the cabin opposite of us had a loud-ish party), the accommodation selections were all excellent that we couldn't even choose until we settled into a 2-bedroom cabin. We visited NRMA in the weekends during school times so there weren't many people so sleep quality would be a top, the park was spotlessly clean, a thumbs up for the park staff. The only negatives was probably the Wi-Fi, I'm not sure but every BIG4 park I've been to has really bad Wi-Fi so can't complain too much and that I only brought my laptop for some homework which didn't require Wi-Fi but was a problem for updates and researching. The giant jumping pillow, bikes were also pretty fun, games room had some decent gaming booths and a pool table, they had a decent sized pool, but we got the whole pool for ourselves cuz there was barely anyone in the park :D.||Overall, location 4/5, atmosphere 4.5/5, rooms 5/5, value (not sure), cleanliness 4.7/5, noise level 4/5 (loud neighbours but mostly quiet), service 4.5/5 and amenities 4.2/5. NRMA Ballarat Holiday Park, you've done excellent.
Had a lovely visit here for 3 nights. Well maintained park with all the facilities a little shed but very clean.

Our cabin was modern & spotlessly clean. Sufficient real milk provided. Good shower. Comfortable bed. Really well sound proof. Suggestion for improvement would be panel blinds on sliding door to eliminate gap in middle from 2 roller blinds. Minutes from Sovereign Hill & Eureka Centre

First time at park, We Booked the powered premium site! First impressions of the park were great, looked lovely and clean with great facilities and amenities and then we noticed the dump station, RIGHT AT OUR DOORSTEP! Sitting there out in the open in an old flaking paint timber outhouse. So wondering how on earth could you call that site PREMIMUM! I do hope that with all the works going on at other sites you have included shifting the dump station, and fixing the potholes would be great too. I am sure you do have all this in the pipe line but we won’t be booking and paying for a premium site again.

Amazing the ladies in reception were very attatentive to our needs and very informative about the area. Great parks for kids even in the rain the kids have an undergraound play area and a heated spa/pool as well. We were traviling in a caravan
Thankyou.

Good location but absolutely zero internet.
We were booked in for 5 nights but left after the first day to relocate to a better park.
Great location, quiet and well maintained

Unfortunately we went and stayed here was supposed to be a 3 day trip Unfortunately we left the next day due to being uncomfortable inside the 3 bedroom deluxe double story villa that is definitely not family friendly expecially if you have young kids upon walking into the cabin the floors where not vacuumed or moped i walked in and instantly has sticky feet there was dust everywhere the bedding was not clean shower was not cleaned properly there is a 2 seater couch that a family of 5 is supposed to sit on to enjoy a movie night I paid over 900$ for the 3 nights here in that cabin and was really let down and we came home the next day stains all over the furniture fridge was not wiped out properly microwave was not wiped out properly not to mention there are potholes all throughout the park that you have to try and avoid the first pot hole starts at the start of the drive way then as you drive throughout the park you will find alot more. I was looking forward to coming to relax on the couch and enjoy a movie night with my kids and partner but unfortunately we were not able to that and were able to do this more comfortably at our own house all kids bedrooms were up on the second floor witch I did not quiet get and there were no baby gates at the top or bottom of the stairs the type of money these places do make is incredible and the fact I payed 300$ and somthing per night was a let down comparing to other 300$ accmodations ive walked into and have gone wow look at this to be fair the staff members were reasonably nice except one I dont know her name tho they were happy to come back to clean but upon further inspection going to get cooking utensils of course was dirty also need up finding a wrapper of a chocolate left in one of the draws to think I wont be refunded or compensated in any way either is really poor not to mention also if its raining you can't go out and sit under cover on the patio because there is nothing covering it so what is the point in having a table and 2 chairs there definitely very let down and I dont think I could possibly go back to this place definitely not with my family also to lock and unlock the door from the inside you need to yous the key witch is a massive saftey problem considering if there was a fire and you can't find your key you only have afew seconds before the door handle becomes to hot to touch or what if you can't find keys your literally trapped inside as there is no safe escape route all together i am very dissapointed and upset about this as we dont get away alot and think after this time I dont think we will go away was a once in a while trip just to be let down and ripped off big time please consider this review before booking wish I had of looked myself have been afew other reviews that were not good either BTW the lady who checked us out was unconcerned about the reason of our early departure bye bye 900$ for a uncomfortable time 😓 walls were filthy aswell
Great park. I door play centre. Good location and set up. Recommended for families.
Lovely clean, tidy park. Friendly accommodating service.

We camped and it was great. Only one night there was some sort of party at the camp kitchen that kept going until after 1am. It really made for an unsettled night for the kids.
Overall though I would come back again.

Fabulous park to stay at if wanting to visit Sovereign Hill and Ballarat area.
Lovely Staff, helpful, friendly and informative.
Good clean amenities, grounds well maintained.
Rates reasonable considering the location.
Visiting relatives staying here
Lovely caravan park, well maintained grounds and facilities, smaller park than most but definitely had everything.

Awesome caravan park
Excellent amenities and lovely staff and close to main Street and great mountain biking trails

Great location to Sovereign Hill.
Small caravan sites, amenities are ok, nice indoor heated spa.
On the pricey end of the scale.

Absolutely  the best NRMA park we have ever stayed. This should be the template for parks in Australia. Immaculate  grounds  all level sites. Most cheerful staff. Always there to offer help when required. Definitely  will be back for a longer stay
Great showers had an ensuite

We spent one night in Ballarat en route to Adelaide. The NRMA park was a perfect place to stop over. We had a studio cabin. It was sparkling clean. A pleasant surprise was the lights were left on, the heating was switched on, even the TV was on. The bed was very comfortable, and it was a very quiet location. The late check-in was a breeze. If we had more than one night in Ballarat, we would have been more than happy to stay for an extended period. We'll definitely be back. ||

Just stayed one night on our way to SA. We arrived quite late. The lights were on, the heater was going, even the TV had been turned on. The whole place is sparkling clean, and late check-in was a breeze. 5 stars all the way.
Excellent caravan Park.. friendly staff.. incredibly clean park and amenities.. well done..
Great

We recently had 3 nights in an accessible cabin, being in a wheelchair, & were very happy with the layout. We used the laundry which was clean, & appliances worked well.
Staff were friendly & helpful.

Great venue, the kids had a ball especially riding the buggy around and the games room. A couple of things though because the place isn’t that cheap. Fix the potholes everywhere would be great, also make the kids beds, clean the poo around the pool even though it’s too cold to use it’s not a nice look, spa could do with a fresh paint, and FYI the air hockey doesn’t accept tokens so beware if purchasing tokens. Otherwise great!

A nice clean and tidy park in the centre of Ballarat.
The new deluxe ensuite sites are good,  but the bathrooms could have done with some type of heating and the caravan aite was difficult to reverse our caravan into.

Second time staying here because we have kids and they like the facilities. We stayed at miner cabin the first time and deluxe cabin this time. Both times find the cleanliness really needs to improve especially the floor. I found the miner cabin very dusty and thought maybe it is a bit old. I was very excited about the deluxe this time, unfortunately we checked in with very dirty floor! No sight of being vacuumed with debris around and apparently it was not regularly mopped and there were ants too.
As my kids love playing on the floor, I spent some time cleaning it and was shocked with how dirty (black) it was. The couch needs steam clean too, full of dirty stains.
We love the spaciousness and quality of the deluxe cabin, very modern with dishwasher. Really wish the place was cleaner. We will be reluctant to come back again.

This is a great little caravan park. We spent four nights here recently in an ensuite site. The park is really well located, close to Sovereign Hill and walking distance to a number of good quality pubs, restaurants and cafes. The facilities at the camp cater to everyone with a small pool, playgrounds both indoors and out, an excellent camp kitchen, a spa and various recreational options. During our stay some damage was caused to one of the amenities blocks by younger people staying at the park. Although the block was closed for a short period of time due to the damage the situation was brilliantly managed by management and communication to all of us staying there was excellent. Despite the inconvenience caused to management and staff I don’t believe the matter impacted significantly on anyone staying at the park. Next time we’re in Ballarat we will definitely be booking into the NRMA Ballarat.

Great location,
Nice and clean, large rooms. Good facilities on site, especially the warm pool and play areas.
Probably a touch expensive for what you get, but still
A great place none the less. Nice and close to sovereign hill and activities. And nice reatraunts
Very good but bring your own 4g internet and coffee capsules!
Experience  better than the Big 4 we stayed in
Stayed here in the motorhome for 10 nights. Friendly staff. Clean amenities. Quiet at night. Would stay here again.

Very friendly staff
Really very helpful.
Lovely spot.
Thankyou very much for your help
Won't be forgotten
Compact well maintained  park. Very clean facilities. Indoor  heated pool as well as outdoor pool. Clean laundry, washers, and dryers and clothes lines. Cement  slabs. Near attractions.

Cabin was not cleaned well at all. We ended up removing all mattresses and banging them out due to the dirt matter left on them. The couch was filthy. Hair and mess all over the pillow. I had to use a brush and shovel to first the whole thing down. The cleaning here is far below my normal NRMA experience. I won't be here again.

This booking was under my partners name so I am not listed as the contact
Excellent park.  Would recommend

Such an amazing park and so well equipped. Unfortunately for us the gaming consoles in the gaming room meant the the kids of our group were constantly wanting to go in there and play, it really detracted from the weekend. Gaming rooms are great if they have pool tables, table tennis, board games even a tv/movie style set up, but we really want to avoid gaming tech as much as possible so I’d encourage the park management to reconsider the presence of the three PlayStations.
The kids have heaps of other awesome park features to use - jumping pillow, swimming pool, swim spa, playgrounds, biking gear, sensory/neurodiversity regulation room.
An unexpected but great feature of the park was the presence of two family bathrooms which included baths, brilliant for little kids.
Lovely staff, nice park, but wasn't able to stay longer due to busy period. 😔
這是一個露營區，我們住的是礦工小屋，裡面可以住六個人，小小的但什麼東西都有，很溫馨，但霉味很重，我一進去就過敏，房內地板很髒建議自己帶拖鞋，早晚溫差非常大房內有暖氣可以使用
Excellent customer service..polite..friendly..both office and park workers
Well situated in Ballarat. Amenities were extremely clean, and the showers were hot. Staff were very helpful and friendly. We will definitely come back

NRMA Ballarat Holiday Park is a great place to go, for kids and adults! At NRMA Ballarat Holiday Park there are many activities for the kids who want to have some fun. Some of them are The jumping pillow, the pool, the arcade, the playground and more!
And for the adults who just want a peaceful and quiet trip with a nice place to go outside and admire all of the nature, this is a perfect place to do that! The workers here are very friendly and nice, and in the reception room there is an ice-cream fridge for days when it gets a bit hot. Overall, this place is amazing and I think you should really come to this place with family, friends or  whoever else you want to take.

Wir waren 4 Nächte auf dem Campingplatz. Der Platz ist toll. Die Stellplätze sind groß und geräumig. Die Sanitäranlagen sauber und ordentlich. Die Waschmaschinen sind ausreichend vorhanden und der Wäscheplatz ist gut. Man muss nicht unbedingt mit dem Trockner arbeiten. 4 Dollar pro Waschmaschine ist auch okay.|Super ist die Lage, Sovereign Hill kann man bequem zu Fuß erreichen. Wir waren sehr zufrieden und haben uns wohlgefühlt.

The room felt very welcoming and comfortable.  The bed felt like a cloud. Very clean.
Only problem was finding somewhere to eat and ended up ad Macdonnolds.
A very quiet park, great staff and amenities. Will definitely visit again
Great location walking distance to Sovigen hill good amentities
We just had the most fun weekend at this park. We loved all the facilities for kids as well as the pool. We will definitely be back!

Great staff
and amenities. Ensuites available on many powered sites.

Booked accommodation just at the right time and V at a great price and being a nrma price arrived at Reception to check in the staff member at front desk was extremely nice gave us our key luclky we stayed here few times and know the layout of the park as we stayed in the same cabin before was  looking forward to a swim but the weather was bad  the cabin was xtremly cabin like aways thanks to the great staff y have employed. Just a bit if  banging next foor late at night left on departure the same staff member was greeted us this morning  as we returned the cabin key we will be back again
Helpful an very friendly service  upon arrival as arrived cold an wet , can not fault my stay here , I can already tell .
Convenient location and excellent service. Great amenities for the kids. Accommodation was clean & tidy,  though mattresses on bunks due for an update.

Over rated got put next to jumping pillow noise All the time should not have site's there.
No sullage Just wrong site not level it should be for what they changed.
I have had better free camp spots than there
Would not recommend

Room didn't feel worth the money but the park itself was really nice.
Ev charger didn't work.
Everything you need in a caravan park for you and the kids. Great location if you are visiting Sovereign Hill.

Walking distance to Soverign Hill. Pizza Hut 500m walk south.
Red Hotel 500m walk north.
GREAT for kids, but not 🥰 for couple.

An amazing holiday park all round! Lots of activities for the kids to do whether it be indoors or outdoors. Customer service was impeccable and so were the rooms. We enjoyed our holiday so much here with lots of family attractions close by.

My husband TIA and I stayed with our dog and van with an en suite site. We loved this location,,leafy green shady trees,concrete slab and close to everything. It was peaceful,our neighbours were great, and the staff very friendly and accommodating. Definitely reccomnend staying here,and we'll come stay again. Thank you for a wonderful stay. Oh and our en suite shower was amazing,big,great water pressure with room for 2. Check in and check out was a breeze with friendly service.👍👍😊😊
Located near Sovereign Hill. Neat & tidy park

Sites had concrete slabs. The area beside the slab was dirt with long weeds  and cigarette butts scattered every where.
The camp kitchen was a mess from the weekend users, with full bins still sitting next to the doorway and outside sitting area even after two days.
Around the bbq area on the floor was bbq grease. This area was also over taken with weeds.
The amenities are aged and could do with a refresh.
The pool area had lots of duck poo on the edges.
Great park, super friendly and helpful staff. Clean and well maintained amenities

How many people(bookings) do you lose by having that ridiculous 2 night booking rule. We want to go to an event around the corner in Larter St but are not prepared to give you an extra night. Will be booking somewhere else.

Shame they don’t seem to get the size right when booking in told them I have a 25ft motorhome I get a site way too small with only half a slab and a fire house invading my space when putting out the awning and a tree/bush in way of the other side window and storage places hose/ water tank etc all good if it was a cheap park 🤷‍♀️but at $53 a night a bit poorly planned snd then there were tinny caravans etc on massive sites no logic
Amenities were good but yeah need to lift there game a bit

Stayed 3 nights in a caravan.
Lovely, clean park.
Toilets, Showers Spotless.
Villa in a caravan park. Great location close to everything.  Activities for the kids - pool, karts, jumping pillow etc

This place is in a good location, not far from town centre and Sovereign Hill. |We stayed in a 2 bedroom cabin, which was clean but basic and the inner walls between bedrooms are very thin. You can easily hear your travel mate snoring in the next room! |There's an ant problem though, we left food such as crackers on the counter, wrapped up so as to avoid them becoming stale, and no food scraps at all, no open packets, and returned to ants all over the kitchen counter. ||It was too cold to swim in the outdoor pool, but it looked lovely. The indoor spa pool was nice and warm and relaxing. The staff were friendly.

Have stayed at this cabin park several times and absolutely love it...staff are very friendly and cabins very clean when we arrived...very family friendly park with jumping pillow..go carts..games room...spa and the pool is really good... will definitely book there again...

We stayed one night in villa 2 bedroom unit for 3 adults.
Happy with rooms BUT very disappointed that we were given 1 bath towel  for 3 adults and we had to use hand towel to dry off.
The bathmat wasn't suitable as a towel either. Office was closed so no opportunity to get more towels.
Complained next morning and was told "sorry" but I think the price was overated

Not sure I’m a fan of the one room bedroom with a double sliding door right off the bed… might have been happier if there was no gap in the blinds too
But it was clean and warm

We rang, asked if they were busy...No was the reply.  We booked in.  Vans start rolling in, and then 2 bus loads of school kids!
First thing which wasn't working was the boom gates....so there goes security.
Weather was crap, went to camp kitchen, only a toaster & kettle, no pots, pans, cups, plates or knives & forks.   Next thing not working was the Coffee machine.
One washing machine and 2 dryers weren't working. The dryers are card only and not real user friendly to those who are not phone savvy.
Used the amenities block. One of the soap dispensers empty even so they clean and check the blocks on a daily basis.
Went over to the camp kitchen this morning to make a cup of coffee....no power to the wall plugs.
We decided with this van park as it was close to soverign Hill.

What a find! Glad we were here in the off peak, as it was tranquil and relaxing. As a couple in a campervan, we splurged on an ensuite site...Wow! It's VERY luxe for a caravan park. Modern and clean, great showers and hot water. Beautiful grounds, the cabins look very nice too. Pet friendly, but make the call first (internet booking system was a bit dicky, so we phoned to book). Walking distance to Sovereign Hill. Red Lion pub just 200m up the road for a good feed. Camp kitchen looks well organised too.
Stayed in a cabin which was modern and very well presented

Location was great & the lady we spoke to in reception was very friendly. Our cabin shower was awful, went from scorching hot to ice cold and no pressure at all making it very hard to shower & impossible for kids as when it went hot, it was extremely hot. We notified reception just after arrival to be told maintenance would look at it the next morning as it's been an issue with 3 of the cabins...not sure if maintenance came at all (we never seen anyone) but no changes to the shower at all the next day leaving us to have to shower in the amenities block while it poured rained going back & forth. Never were we offered another cabin even though some were available.
One Of The Best Caravan Parks On Australia!
Staff very helpful with booking arriving next week

2大2小入住deluxe studio 2晚
房內衛浴乾淨寬敞，停車方便。
園區公共衛浴設施乾淨足夠！
下次再訪Ballarat ,首選住宿！

Only stayed a few nights but was good for the kids and ideally located near Sovereign Hill and close to two pubs and shops.|Jumping Pillow was great and loved the spa nice and warm on a freezing day|Confusing prices when booked on line and then had to cancel one child and the staff|were confused at the computer system they had to work with.|Games room machines played up a bit
The only thing might be area is internet

I stayed at this caravan park for a week n a caravan site in a new 2nd hand van I had purchased. During my stay I needed gas as my gas bottle was empty. I attended the office to purchase a ‘swap n go’ bottle – I paid $38 and swapped my grey coloured non-swap n go bottle with a swap n go bottle. The young lady who served me was delightful and knows good customer service. When I took the gas bottle back to my caravan, I realised the connection was not the right connection and would not fit my van and I immediately returned the gas bottle within 2 to 3 minutes with the security plug and sticker intact. Then I was confronted by another older aggressive lady in the office who was like a pit bull and probably better suited to being a night club bouncer than working in customer service. She barked at me that I can’t get a refund (despite consumer laws) as the bottle had been used / security sticker removed (all her assumptions as she had not been involved until this point). At all interactions with the pit bull, I remained calm, polite and reasonable. I explained that the bottle had not been tampered with or any gas used and that the issue was it is the wrong connection for my van. I told her that the other girl had placed the bottle back into the sales cage intact on the shelf with the other gas bottles ready to sell. She told me to come back the next day and she will see if I can get a refund. The next day the pit bull told me that the park manager was an ogre, and she would not give a refund as the bottle had been tampered with (which again I attempted to explain it had not). Despite consumer law, the pit bull advised me that the ogre park manager decided (on the word of the pit bull) that I will not get a refund despite the bottle being placed back into the sales cage intact and on the shelf with all the other intact gas bottles ready to be sold. Essentially, this park manager will make twice the income from the one bottle as it will be sold to another person – a total scam! The pit bull initially pretended that she didn’t know how to give refunds and kept telling me to come back the next day, treating me Ike I was a joke to her.  The only thing the pit bull appeared to be interested in was overtly flirting with all the young tradies who come into the office (while I was waiting in there) while they stand awkwardly tolerating her unsolicited advances.
I have made a report to Consumer Affairs Victoria who are investigating the actions of this park manager and their refusal to provide a lawful refund. This park manager runs this caravan park like a dictator overseeing her little kingdom of domination with no regard to consumer laws or doing what is right by their customers. I will never return to this caravan park (for this and other reasons), nor would I recommend it. It's interesting how I received a threatening email from this park manager after letting her know I will be making an honest review - a keyboard warrior who couldn't be bothered to speak with me while staying at the park for a week! The email has been passed onto the police.
**update** Consumer Affairs Victoria confirmed that the manager has breached consumer law - the NRMA head office refunded my money, despite the caravan park managers actions.

We're in a caravan with ensuite. The staff have been wonderful, the ensuite is fantastic and the location is great too. We are really happy with our choice to stay here for our weekend away. Thanks Sue 😘
Stayed in Ballarat for work in a studio cabin, staff were very accommodating with me arriving later than the check in time. Studio cabins are modern and clean

We had an ensuite site (site 31), a good sized site with a paved pad for the caravan. The ensuite was really good, nice shower and plenty of room. Camp kitchen was very nice, well equipped. Plenty to do in the park for kids with pedal carts and a good size playground. Only complaint was the poor quality wi-fi.

We had a mid week stay which was lovely, we were staying in one of the villa which was nice and comfortable size, closed to everything that we needed.
Couple of things I was concerned about was the black mould in the shower maybe because there is no one in there for a while and the cabin haven’t been air out with the condensation that might have cause it!? The cleanliness need to pay abit more attention on the corner and skirting as well. Just didn’t look good. Apart from that we are happy and thank you for having us.

Good space for a camper trailer, has good grass and is easy to get around the park.
En-suite was a bonus given it was a bit of a wet night.
Will be back for sure

This is a clean and tidy park if you’re looking for somewhere to stay while in Ballarat. |Good facilities and easy access. |The sites are large enough to accommodate vans and there’s a pool, jumping pillow and bikes for the kids. |It is pricey though at $57 pn and when we stayed there was a minimum 3 night stay which was costly as we only wanted two.
Nice park but pricey. Also had a 3 night minimum when we only needed 2 nights so ended up costing us $170 for 2 nights on a powered site 😑

เป็นแคมป์ไซท์ที่ดูดีมาก สะอาด ใหม่ ที่จอดรถบ้านมีแบบ ensuit ให้เลือกด้วย (มันคือห้องน้ำส่วนตัวสำหรับเราคนเดียว ประมาณว่าเช่าห้องน้ำ1ห้องเลย) ที่อื่นไม่ค่อยมีห้องน้ำส่วนตัวให้เลือก

แนะนำครับ
Great place to stay, lots of facilities. Clean accommodation.  The staff were really friendly.  Very close to sovereign hill.

Family enjoyed stay here. Pool, jumping pillow, playground, play room all great. Staff very helpful. Cabin was cleaned well, but needs a deep clean. Walls very dirty with old liquids and grime. Sheets were not clean.Overall though, very nice stay

We stayed in a 3 bedroom deluxe villa. Not only did we get an amazing price for our room, but then they upgraded us too! Quite large and Very comfortable. Beds were great, doona was cosy as. Overall very happy with our accommodation. Lots of activities available if you have kids. Very friendly staff. I can’t fault the place.

Amazing amazing amazing!!, after having to leave fire area we were welcomed with open arms with discounts to help us all out  . The children received lollie bags and had perfect time swimming,  riding and playing in games rooms and playgrounds and not having to worry  about what could be at home . would highly recommend for a family holiday .

Very tight park with uneven sites.

Wouldn’t stay again
Rooms small considering price
great pool and spa Bonus

Fußläufig von Sovereign Hill entfernt.
Campelstellplätze zwar eng, aber die Nacht von Sonntag auf Montag war ruhig.
Den Pool ab 18.00 sehr genossen.
Sanitäre Einrichtungen waren etwas älter, aber sehr sauber und ordentlich.

Two weeks ago the experience was excellent   It was very busy been holidays  but still very peacefulwe s wonderful stat reception was great the last vist this was excellent  we stay in a deluxe cabin and the patrons next door had music up all night and loud

Highlight was the sensory room. Maintenance could be improved as all taps in family bathroom leaked, were hard to turn off and simply needed new washers. When reported to reception they were aware and waiting on a plumber. It shouldn't take over a week to change washers.

This a great park.  Amenities always  kept spotless by the wonderful Carol.  What a delight this lady is - always easy with a smile - and always working at top speed.  This park delivers all that you could need.  Especially impressed with “Reilly’s Room” for children that need a quiet space ..

Great clean park
Staff especially Sarah was wonderful.
Very pleasant and helpful

Staff were very friendly and helpful given our circumstances. Had to make last minute booking due to car issues, the next day was hot, and staff allowed a late checkout while our car was being fixed, even gave the kids an icy-pole. very accommodating and we will be back when we next visit Ballarat.

We just checked out today after a 7 night stay and I must say I felt like I needed to leave a review for these guys! Absolutely amazing staff. Our 2 bedroom cabin was perfect for what we needed (2 adults and 1 child) and we went with 8 other families as an annual trip we do each year.

This year we booked a little late and we only found NRMA Ballarat that was able to cater for us all and they sure did come through with the goods. They had activities everyday for the kids, the pool was awesome for all ages. All other areas of the park were also really good.

We also met the park manager Susan who was absolutely lovely and always made sure we felt at home.

Let’s just say that, to keep all 9 families happy during a 7 night stay pretty much sums it all up. We will definetly be back and would highly recommend this site for anyone. Amazing work NRMA team!

My family and I just returned from a week’s long stay at the NRMA holiday park in Ballarat and I have to say we thoroughly enjoyed our stay.
From the friendliness and hospitality of the staff to the effort put in to the daily activities scheduled for the kids, we had the most amazing time bonding with our extended family in the park.
As a family of 4, we stayed in the 2 bedroom cabin which caters to your basic needs (no oven in kitchen, stove only).
Coin Laundry and communal kitchen are also on site if need be.
There is an indoor heated pool and a lovely outdoor pool.
We enjoyed playing tennis, basketball and riding on the carts. The kids especially loved the games room.
The park is just a stones throw from town and Sovereign Hill is only a few minutes walk.
Sue and Team were fabulous. We cannot wait to return for our next family holiday.

Good location, great amenities for families with kids and a good location to the main attractions throughout Ballarat. Staff for a bit average in their service. One example of me asking for change for the laundry facilities and was told that they can’t give me change even though they were counting it on the counter and I could see it quite clearly. Just lazy staff relying on text message park updates over communicating directly with customers.

I would go again though as the park has good facilities for young families and walkable to sovereign hill.

We have just come back from a short stay at the caravan park, and we were very impressed. We stayed in a cabin, with 3 bedrooms and it was a great size, with all the necessary things for a comfortable home away from home stay. Even though they had some issues with other visitors, the staff stayed professional and made sure no one else was affected. Well done team.

We are at NRMA BIG 4 holiday Park. We were told all facilities will be open on 25 December inside but there is no one at the reception. Indoor spa is closed.  So called heated Swimming pool is very dirty and not heated.  Carts are not working except one that worked yesterday. Jumping pillow is closed. Tennis court and basket ball courts are super small and not maintained. It's a safety hazard with rusty old equipment lying in the kids play area. This has been a complete rip off.

Nice caravan park, close to local attractions. It was quiet when I stayed even with families and two coach loads worth of school campers. I camped on powered grass site 39, nice shady spot under a tree, close to the amenities and camp kitchen which were both spotless my entire stay. The only downside is that the power for sites 39 & 40 are quite a distance away, I couldn’t make sense of why? So, I had to go and buy a bigger extension cord just to get power to reach my spot (would suggest a 20m cord at minimum). Camp kitchen was great, better than most I’ve seen. I would stay here again.
Lovely place to stay, very clean, very well looked after, only down fall its a bit pricey but other then that it worth it
Great family friendly park. Excellent facilities and walking distance to Sovereign Hill
Ok but prices are rude they charge on a sliding scale the busier they are the more it costs RUDE and should be illegal
Great facilities for kids, friendly staff and dog friendly

We stayed on a powered sight and what a wonderful experience. Lovely, clean, safe camp ground....not too big which was easy for small children. Lovely playground, indoor play area, huge jumping pillow, resort like pool and clean camp kitchen and toilet and showers. Best part was that the staff were all lovely and friendly and went out of their way to help. We will definitely be back. Thank you 😊

Pricing structure like all break down companies buying parks is a total price gouge was charged on a sliding scale because they said that they were busy but the park was 30% empty caravan parks association needs to clamp down on these practices fixed price all year round like it used to be.
Great location, very helpfull staff

A great little van park close to town and attractions, good clean facilities, well kept, quiet  and tidy.  The good bits are very good !!!

The down side is the van sites are very small and many are near impossible for larger vans, the staff don't seem to understand the needs of vanners well and don't offer any assistance or check the size of the van for the site they have booked for it. :-(

we booked the new onsuite site, the facilities are great but parking the van was an effort. I saw one van owner damage a new car while trying to reverse, another had to give up and demand a site that fitted their van.

(Rainbow beach in QLD has solved this problem with a small tractor called "The Marriage Saver" and everyone who stays has their van parked for them !!!

They have installed a new cashless laundry dryer system and the office staff have no idea how it works, again for people who do not use moble phones to pay the system is near  impossible to use.

They have a small heated pool I was looking forward to relaxing in after a long drive but it's out of order and closed :-(

No one seems to know anything about what is going on.

It's a good place ....... just mildly annoying stuff stops it from being great

------------------------------------------------------------------------------

NOTE : The slab for the van was great but by the time we managed to back the van onto it it was very muddy after heavy rain and fresh topsoil

It was quick and easy to wash down with a hose ....... the only problem was trying to decide if I should was the mud away or pan for gold :-) !!!
It was a very good park and very central to the town center.  We met really nice people next to us a huge family.  I recommend this park.
Lovely place. Close to the Sovereign Hill site and not far from the city centre.
Stayed on a powered site. Very awkward to get vans onto sites, particularly bigger vans and rigs. Amenities good.
Had a great time. Good amenities. Only flaw is having to pay to use spa. Otherwise, enjoyed everything else.

Highly recommend this caravan park if you have a family. It's in a great location,  not far from the town.
Swimming pool, playground, and pedal bikes for the kids, it was great. The weather was fantastic when we were there, and the kids were always doing something around the park.

Only good things can be said about this place. I was reluctant to pay double what I had been at other parks however it felt I got a value for money here. Incredible pool. Their bouncy pillow is huge! Good toilet blocks that are central to all. My only slight feedback would have been would have been great to have access to a couple more outdoor bbqs. Aside from that, its in a remarkable spot and you can walk out to the nearby bakery and even sovereign hill if you so desire. Would definitely be back.
Yep ok

I stayed in my caravan. This park was easy to find and great location.
It’s the first place I’ve stayed where I had my own ensuite with my caravan!!
So impressive to have that situated at the end of my van!
The grounds were a little muddy due to the wet weather but overall it it a fantastic place to stay. Highly recommend

We really enjoyed our stay at the NRMA Ballarat Holiday Park. The two bedroom miners cabin we rented was great. Well appointed with nice little touches like tea, coffee and cookies. There was plenty of space- one bedroom with a double bed and the other with 4 bunks. Living and dining room with TV and generous 6 seat table. Kitchen is was good- full size fridge, hot plate and microwave. Note there is no oven or grill, not that we needed it. There are communal BBQs. Linen and towels are included.
The children's playgrounds, both indoor and outdoor are amazing! Our kids had so much fun on them. There is also a bouncing pillow and pool. You can also rent ebikes and go karts.
The park is walking distance from Sovereign Hill and a 3 minute drive from both Ballarat CBD and the Ballarat Wildlife Park.
Walking distance there are Indian, Chinese and Pizza takeaways along with a bottle shop.
We highly recommend and would stay again.
Great powered sites,close enough to walk to Sovereign Hill😊

Was a great park, heaters in bathrooms, well set out park. Only drawback was 2 school groups there with upto 80 students between both groups. Kids were playing right next to our van when there was plenty of grass areas with no vans on them
Perfect place to stay while visiting 12 apostles. Only 7 mins drive. The town is quaint and the locals are super friendly.

We loved it, clean tidy park & facilities, my twin 5 year old's couldn't get enough of the pools, jumping pillow and many different indoor & outdoor play areas. The staff were friendly & moved about the park busily maintaining it while staying in the background & not bothering the park users. My two didn't even ask for the IPad's for the whole 4 day's. We'll be back.👍

EDIT. We won't be back, NRMA now own this park & their website is very decieving regarding prices (showing a price then you call to book & it's almost tripled because its off peak) but not mentioned as you're searching for accommodation.
It's actually cheaper to stay in a flash Melbourne CBD motel with amazing city views, indoor heated pool, walking distance to restaurants & the only thing my girl's will miss out on is the jumping pillow so we'll go to the Melbourne aquarium instead.
They're excited with our new holiday booking & so am I.
This used to be a favourite of ours but it has unfortunately gone down hill. Cabins need updating. Heater didn't work properly and kitchen tap had no hot water.

Great location for historic getaway for the family, great amenities and service . Only 700m from Sovereign hill .
Cabins, caravans and tent sites .
Heated pool, playground, DVD for entertainment .
Discount for membership 😛
Close to shopping experiences.

We chose to stay at the nrma Ballarat holiday park as it is so close to Sovereign Hill. The park itself is fantastic for kids! We especially loved the spa.... And even better that you get it all to yourself for the hour you book 😀 I unfortunately ended up being admitted to Ballarat Hospital for the week.... So my parents drove from Melbourne to Ballarat to look after my 2 little boys. The staff were just amazing.... They looked after my family, even letting them stay longer despite it being school holidays and a very busy time. I cannot thank them enough! ❤️

Never been to Ballarat before it’s a beautiful place a little cold for us North Queenslanders. The park was great although the caravan site was a little tight for our big van. The prices for the washing machine and dryers were a bit expensive compared to other parks we have stayed. The camp kitchen was excellent and the amenities. Very close to Sovereign Hills. The girls at reception were all very nice and friendly and recommended lots of places to see and eat at.
Great Park
Stayed here for a night in a three bedroom cabin. Service was good. It's nice and close to Sovereign Hill. Cabin had everything we needed. We would stay again.
Super helpful reception staff, good facilities, and close to town. Really enjoyed staying.
